content.problemStatement:
Write a program to extract the English alphabetic characters from a string containing a mixture of lowercase, uppercase, and special characters.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase and uppercase English letters, along with some other characters. (There shall be no space in the string)


Output Format

For each test case, print the string with the only English letters of each string in the same order.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase and uppercase English letters, along with some other characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the string with only the English letters of each string in the same order.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The input string contains letters H e l l o C h e f interleaved with digits and symbols. Keeping only the letters yields "HelloChef".
For each line we drop the digits, leaving only the alphabetic characters.
No alphabetic characters are present, so the output line is empty.

solution.approach: The key insight is to iterate over each character in the input string and check if it is an English letter. This can be done using ASCII values, where 'a' to 'z' have values from 97 to 122 and 'A' to 'Z' have values from 65 to 90. By filtering out characters that do not fall within these ranges, we can extract the English letters from the string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of a string. This is because we iterate over each character in each string once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the filtered string in each test case.

code.Python3:
import sys
for _ in range(int(sys.stdin.readline().strip())):
    s = sys.stdin.readline().strip()
    print(''.join(c for c in s if 'a' <= c <= 'z' or 'A' <= c <= 'Z'))
